text: A high-intensity warm-up including strides at 100% race intensity improves 5000m time trial performance compared to a low-intensity warm-up in trained runners.
A warm-up protocol of 1x500m at 70% race intensity plus 3x250m at 100% race intensity is effective preparation for 5000m racing.
High-intensity warm-up improves pacing strategy during a 5000m time trial compared to low-intensity warm-up.
High-intensity warm-up strides improve countermovement jump performance, indicating enhanced neuromuscular readiness before distance running.
Post-warm-up blood lactate of ~3.5 mmol/L from high-intensity strides does not impair subsequent 5000m performance and may indicate beneficial metabolic priming.
Higher perceived exertion during warm-up from race-pace strides does not negatively affect subsequent 5000m race performance.
